message:
Isolate FrankenEye blink interval into its own useEffect

The blink logic (setInterval with random probability + 120ms
timeout) was previously inlined in the same useEffect as the
IntersectionObserver, mouse-move listener, scroll listener, and
resize listener. Because isBlinking was in the dependency array,
every blink cycle (setIsBlinking(true) → 120ms → setIsBlinking
(false)) triggered a full teardown and re-setup of ALL event
listeners and the observer — roughly every 2.5 seconds.

Fix: extract the blink interval into a dedicated useEffect that
only manages its own setInterval and cleanup timeout. The main
effect now removes isBlinking from its dependency array entirely,
so mouse tracking and intersection observation are registered
once and remain stable across blink cycles.

This eliminates ~400ms of listener churn per blink and prevents
a brief gap where mouse-move tracking was inactive (between the
old effect's cleanup and re-setup).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
